mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures, proxied by nominal retail sales (RSAFS), indicate strengthening consumer demand which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines by more than 1% month-over-month and S&P 500 still rises in the subsequent 20 trading days, or if the correlation between RSAFS and forward returns is statistically insignificant (p>0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals improving labor market conditions, which reduces recession fears and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE declines by 0.3pp triggers and S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in at least 40% of cases, or if the signal's Sharpe ratio over triggered periods is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS near zero) and stable consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL below 3% year-over-year) indicates accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressure, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity prices, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction term amplifies the signal beyond what either series individually conveys.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with low inflation
failure_condition: If multiple linear regression of forward returns on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not show a significant positive interaction term (p<0.05) over any 5-year rolling window, or if the conditional return when both conditions hold is not significantly above average.

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession economic expansion, growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals strengthening manufacturing and supply-side capacity, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. In contrast, during recession regimes, rising industrial production would be negative if it occurs late-cycle.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth above 0.5% month-over-month does not precede positive S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of cases when the regime is expansion (NBER-defined).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 800,000 units per month, it signals a self-sustaining housing recovery that boosts consumer wealth and construction-related employment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after HOUST exceeds 800k, the average S&P 500 60-day return is not positive and significantly different from zero (t-test p>0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) provides a consistent signal of genuine labor market improvement, which supports consumer spending and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the composite signal (PAYEMS increasing and UNRATE decreasing simultaneously) does not yield an average 20-day return above 0.5% with hit rate > 55% over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: During the early-cycle recovery phase of the economy, rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a strong leading indicator of economic expansion, boosting S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. In late-cycle phases, the same rise may signal overheating and be neutral or negative.
failure_condition: If HOUST growth is not a statistically significant predictor of forward 60-day returns during the early-cycle regime (as defined by NBER cycle dating) with a t-statistic > 2.0.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly increase in retail sales (RSAFS) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals immediate economic momentum that lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average forward 5-day return following months with both RSAFS and INDPRO rising is not positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) over a 10-year sample.
